success_criteria: Contract gains ExtractInto []string field with yaml/json tag extract_into,omitempty
validate.go rule 11 extends to validate every ExtractInto entry under existing per-entry rules (no traversal, no absolute, no control chars, no drive letters, no UNC, no zero-width)
validate.go gains rule 17 rejecting extract_into entries with code-file extensions (.go, .py, .ts, .tsx, .js, .md, .yaml, .yml, .json, .toml, .rs, .java, .cpp, .h, .c)
PreToolUse hook reads ETHOS_VERIFIER_EXTRACT_INTO and authorizes Write/Edit to non-existing paths under any listed directory; existing path Write/Edit still requires ETHOS_VERIFIER_ALLOWLIST match
SubagentStart sets ETHOS_VERIFIER_EXTRACT_INTO from m.ExtractInto in the verifier env block
Verifier isolation block lists extract_into alongside write_set with prose distinguishing modify vs create

findWriteSetConflicts extends to the six-rule closed form per DES-052 — ws-file/ws-file (existing), ws-file/ws-dir (existing), ws-dir/ws-dir (existing), ws-file/ei-dir is a conflict iff D is a prefix of P (new), ws-dir/ei-dir is a conflict iff segment-prefix overlap (new), ei-dir/ei-dir never conflicts; relation is symmetric
Archetype struct gains ExtractIntoConstraints []string field; archetype.go enforcement applies the same glob rules as WriteSetConstraints
Seed archetypes updated per DES-052 default table (design → docs/**, test → tests/** + *_test.go, investigate → docs/** + .tmp/**, others empty)
lint.go gains lintMonolithPressure and lintExtractIntoFileEntries warnings per DES-052
Tests cover every new code path — per-entry validation, PreToolUse stat branch, SubagentStart env propagation, admission control table rows, archetype constraint enforcement, lint heuristics
make check passes — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, all tests pass with -race -count=1
make check-oo does not regress (no decline in OO scores on touched files)
CHANGELOG.md gains an Added entry under
Round-by-round summary captured in the mission reflection so the leader can append it when transitioning DES-052 from DRAFT to SETTLED (DESIGN.md is leader-authored; not in this worker's write_set)

Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (schema field, then validation, then PreToolUse, then SubagentStart, then admission, then archetypes, then lint, then archetype YAMLs, then CHANGELOG/DESIGN). Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
ethos mission dispatch accepts --extract-into flag taking comma-separated relative directories; help text names the flag and links DES-052
Empty/omitted --extract-into leaves Contract.ExtractInto as a nil slice — backward compatible
Populated --extract-into entries flow through splitCSV to Contract.ExtractInto and are validated by the same per-entry rules as write_set (validateWriteSetEntry plus rule 17)

cmd/ethos/mission_test.go gains a subtest exercising dispatch with --extract-into — confirms successful create, the created contract carries ExtractInto, and a malformed entry (code-file extension or traversal) is rejected with a clear error
AGENTS.md mission contract YAML example shows extract_into alongside write_set with a one-line annotation distinguishing modify-existing vs create-new
AGENTS.md dispatch example uses --extract-into alongside --write-set

AGENTS.md archetypes section names extract_into_constraints and points at the DES-052 default table in DESIGN.md

auditEntry struct gains parent_session, agent_id, agent_type, delegation_id, parent_delegation, contract_id, tool_input (full), tool_input_hash; all new fields are omitempty so old JSONL parses with new code

Store gains a two-root constructor — NewStoreWithRoots(repoRoot, globalRoot) — and dispatches Create / Load / List / Update / Close / LoadResults / LoadReflections / LogReader through both roots; old NewStore(root) preserved as a thin wrapper for backward compat
Mission files write under <repoRoot>/.ethos/missions/<mission-id>/ when repoRoot is set; fall back to <globalRoot>/missions/<id>.yaml shape when repoRoot is empty
Session audit JSONL writes under <repoRoot>/.ethos/sessions/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<session-id>/audit.jsonl when repoRoot is set; legacy ~/.punt-labs/ethos/sessions/<id>.audit.jsonl read as fallback

NewID gains rollback API — returns (id string, release func(commit bool), error); the per-namespace per-date counter file at ~/.punt-labs/ethos/counters/<namespace>-YYYY-MM-DD is a single-int file with flock-guarded read/inc/write; release(false) decrements; release(true) commits; idempotent if another process already incremented
Mission ID and delegation ID counters live in separate sibling files under ~/.punt-labs/ethos/counters/ — missions-YYYY-MM-DD and delegations-YYYY-MM-DD
JSONL atomic-write contract — f.Sync() after every audit-log line; reader tolerates partial trailing lines (no \n) with stderr warning
Contract YAML decoded with KnownFields(true); auditEntry JSONL decoded permissive — the asymmetry is named in a code comment in audit_log.go

Tests cover schema additions (round-trip with and without new fields), Store two-root dispatch (repo vs global), per-namespace counter atomicity under 10-goroutine concurrent NewID calls, JSONL partial-line recovery, audit-entry full-tool_input persistence, tool_input_hash canonical-JSON stability
AGENTS.md storage layout table updated to show the new two-tree layout (missions/<mission-id>/, sessions/<date>-<session-id>/) and sibling counter file pattern
CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Added entry naming DES-054 phase 1
make check clean; make check-oo does not regress on touched files

Hook command structure — case statement with three branches — bypass .tmp/ and .ethos/ paths, then .go/Makefile/.sh/.yaml/.yml run make check, else exit 0
The exact shell expression in the case statement is the one already deployed locally in .claude/agents/bwk.md (verbatim) so the generator emits what manual patching emitted
Tests in generate_agents_test.go assert the emitted hook command contains the .tmp/ and .ethos/ bypass clause and the .go/.sh/.yaml/.yml trigger clause

Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (auditEntry, Store dispatch, NewID, JSONL atomic-write, tests, docs). Each commit passes make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.

HandleAuditLog emits a sentinel JSONL line {ts, session, audit_error: <reason>} to the audit log when writeAuditEntry fails (fsync error, EACCES, ENOSPC). The sentinel uses the existing path resolution (repo-tree first, legacy fallback); if both paths are unwritable, the stderr warning remains the only signal. New test exercises a permission-denied directory and asserts the sentinel lands.

NewID release(false) emits stderr lines on its two silent no-op branches — unreadable counter (counter read fails) and concurrent-advance (current != allocated). Format: `ethos: id release: counter at N, expected M, skipping decrement` and `ethos: id release: read counter %s: %v`. New tests assert the stderr line on each branch.

pathSetForExisting and the per-artifact helpers (contractPath, logPath, reflectionsPath, resultsPath) return (string, error) so callers distinguish stat errors from genuine not-found. Load / Update / Close / LoadResults / LoadReflections / LogReader call sites updated to propagate the error or wrap it. New test asserts EACCES on a repo-tree contract.yaml surfaces as a wrapped error from Load (not as ErrNotExist).

repoCreateLockPath wired into withCreateLock as a nested flock — acquired AFTER the global createLockPath flock, held for the duration of the Create. The acquisition order is documented inline. New test under temp-dir setup verifies both flocks are held during Create by attempting to acquire each from a sibling goroutine and confirming both block.
make check clean; no suppression of any gate; no nolint, no xfail. All existing tests still pass.
DES-052 extract_into discipline — if any new helper file is needed (e.g., audit_sentinel.go for the failure-emit helper), extract into internal/hook/. Do not bloat audit_log.go past its current size.
Commit incrementally — one commit per fix (4 fixes = 4 commits). Each passes make check before the next begins.

PreToolUse-on-Agent hook implemented in internal/hook/pretooluse.go. Reads hook stdin payload; identifies Tier A vs Tier B per the DES-054 v5 dispatch rules (DESIGN.md §"PreToolUse-on-Agent"): (a) parent contract has delegations[] with matching spawn_pattern → Tier B inheritance; (b) MISSION_ID env set → Tier B explicit; (c) neither → Tier A. Tier A spawn must succeed even when no mission is in scope.
Tier A advice hook emits a one-line stderr suggestion pointing at `mission dispatch` UNLESS ETHOS_QUIET_ADVICE=1 OR PARENT_SESSION_ID is already populated (nested ad-hoc spawn). Test asserts: bare spawn → advice; ETHOS_QUIET_ADVICE=1 → no advice; PARENT_SESSION_ID set → no advice; advice line shape matches the design literal.
Per-mission flock acquired in shared/read mode before any Tier B delegation skeleton write. Per-delegation flock acquired in exclusive mode for the skeleton + prompt.md write. Acquisition order documented inline: global → repo → per-mission(shared) → per-delegation(exclusive). Release reverse via defer LIFO. New test under temp-dir setup verifies the shared/exclusive interleaving: two concurrent Tier B spawns under the same mission both acquire the shared mission flock; their per-delegation flocks do not contend.
Session-flock unification: one flock now covers BOTH the session roster AND the session audit log. Documented inline that this eliminates the prior two-lock acquisition order. Existing session.Store callers updated; existing audit append path takes the unified lock.
Env propagation on Agent spawn: DELEGATION_ID and PARENT_SESSION_ID always set; MISSION_ID and MISSION_ARTIFACTS_DIR set only for Tier B. The propagation point is the PreToolUse hook's response (env block in the hook stdout JSON, per Claude Code hook protocol). Test asserts the JSON shape for both tiers.
max_delegation_depth refusal closes the just-written Tier B delegation skeleton with verdict=aborted and closed_at=<now>. The depth is read from .punt-labs/ethos.yaml (default 16) via the existing config loader; depth is computed by walking the parent_delegation chain. Test asserts: depth 17 → refusal + skeleton closed with aborted; depth 16 → spawn proceeds; depth 1 → spawn proceeds.
Hash-gate refusal aborted-sentinel cleanup: when SubagentStart's evaluator-hash check (DES-033) refuses a Tier B verifier spawn AFTER the delegation skeleton has been written, the refusal path now writes verdict=aborted and closed_at=<now> to the record. No orphaned skeletons. Test asserts the verdict on the skeleton after a forced hash mismatch.
DES-052 extract_into discipline followed. Any helper extracted for the dispatch routing (e.g. parent-contract resolver, spawn-pattern matcher) lands in a new file under internal/hook/ or internal/mission/, never inlined into an existing god module. Cap on file growth: no existing file in the write_set grows by more than 200 lines.
make check clean — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, all tests with -race -count=1, validate-content. No nolint. No xfail. No suppression of any gate.
Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step (file group, single concern, or single PR-equivalent slice). Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
CHANGELOG.md entry added under ## [Unreleased] in the Added section. DESIGN.md updated only to mark phase 2 implemented (no design re-litigation).

pretooluse.go HandlePreToolUse: when tool_name == "Agent" AND MISSION_ID env is set, dispatch as Tier B. Resolve the mission via existing mission.Store.Load; allocate a new delegation_id via mission.NewID with namespace="delegations"; emit the delegation_id in the hook response env block. Tier A path (when MISSION_ID empty) preserves the round 3 advice behavior unchanged.
delegation.go gains exclusive-flock helper (e.g. AcquireDelegationLock(repoRoot, delegationID) returning a release func + error). The helper opens <repo>/.ethos/delegations/<delegation-id>.lock with LOCK_EX, returns a closure that runs LOCK_UN + Close. No public Delegation skeleton writer yet — that's a later mission.
Hook response includes env block setting DELEGATION_ID (always when Agent), MISSION_ID (only when Tier B, echo from input env), PARENT_SESSION_ID (always when Agent — read from input session_id). Format the response per Claude Code hook protocol (json with continue=true and additional_env).
Tests in pretooluse_test.go cover: Agent with MISSION_ID env set → response has additional_env with MISSION_ID and DELEGATION_ID; Agent without MISSION_ID → no MISSION_ID in response, advice on stderr (round 3 behavior preserved); non-Agent tool → unchanged passthrough; AcquireDelegationLock helper acquires + releases without error on tempdir; double- acquire from sibling goroutine blocks until first release.
DES-052 extract_into discipline. If a new helper file is needed (e.g. internal/hook/pretooluse_dispatch.go), create it; do NOT bloat pretooluse.go past +150 lines this round.
make check clean — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, all tests with -race -count=1, validate-content. No suppression.
Commit incrementally — one commit per logical step. Each commit must pass make check. Do not accumulate more than 30 minutes of uncommitted changes.
Close ceremony FIRST. Write the result.yaml and reflect.yaml template at the start of the session and submit them via `ethos mission result` and `ethos mission reflect` BEFORE content work is finalized — this is the lesson from rounds 1-2 of the previous mission, which terminated mid-cascade without ceremony.

delegation.go gains WriteDelegationSkeleton(repoRoot, missionID, delegationID string, payload DelegationSkeleton) error. Writes <repo>/.ethos/missions/<mission-id>/delegations/<NN>/record.yaml with verdict=open, opened_at=<now>, plus the caller-supplied fields (parent_delegation, agent_type, prompt_hash). Mkdir the parent dir 0o700. The DelegationSkeleton struct lives in delegation.go and is yaml-tagged.
delegation.go gains AcquireMissionLock(repoRoot, missionID string) (release func(), err error) — acquires LOCK_SH (shared read) on <repo>/.ethos/missions/<mission-id>/.lock. Documented inline that this complements AcquireDelegationLock (exclusive) so two Tier B spawns under the same mission both hold the shared mission lock concurrently while their per-delegation locks do not contend. New test asserts two concurrent shared acquisitions both succeed; an exclusive acquisition (LOCK_EX) waits.
dispatchTierB in pretooluse_dispatch.go now: (1) acquires mission shared lock, (2) acquires per-delegation exclusive lock, (3) writes the delegation skeleton, (4) emits the env block including MISSION_ARTIFACTS_DIR set to the per-delegation dir (<repo>/.ethos/missions/<id>/delegations/<NN>). Release order LIFO via defer.
Tests cover: Tier B dispatch writes record.yaml at the expected path; MISSION_ARTIFACTS_DIR appears in response env; concurrent Tier B spawns under one mission both acquire shared lock and write their respective per-delegation dirs; a sibling goroutine holding LOCK_EX on the mission lock blocks the dispatch until released.
DES-052 extract_into discipline. New helper file in internal/hook/ (e.g. pretooluse_skeleton.go) or internal/mission/ (e.g. delegation_skeleton.go) if needed to keep file growth within +200 lines per existing file.
make check clean. No suppression.
Commit incrementally. Each commit passes make check.
Close ceremony FIRST. Write result.yaml and reflect.yaml templates early in the session; submit them before the session ends. The previous mission (m-2026-05-22-030) only narrowly completed ceremony due to leader intervention at the 16-minute mark. Don't rely on intervention.

delegation.go gains CloseDelegationSkeleton(repoRoot, missionID, delegationID, verdict, closedAt string) error. Reads the existing record.yaml, updates verdict and closed_at, rewrites atomically (tempfile + rename). Verdict argument must be one of DelegationVerdict{Pass,Fail,Error,Aborted}.
dispatchTierB in pretooluse_dispatch.go: after acquiring locks and writing the skeleton, COMPUTE the proposed delegation depth by walking the parent_delegation chain (use existing Delegation load + ParentDelegation field). If depth would exceed mission/resolve.ResolveMaxDelegationDepth, close the just-written skeleton via CloseDelegationSkeleton with verdict=aborted, return a structured error in the hook response (continue=false, reason="max_delegation_depth N exceeded by depth M"), and DO NOT propagate the env block. Test: depth N → aborted close, no env propagated; depth N-1 → spawn proceeds normally.
subagent_start.go: the existing DES-033 evaluator-hash check that refuses a Tier B verifier spawn AFTER the delegation skeleton has been written now closes the skeleton via CloseDelegationSkeleton with verdict=aborted before returning the refusal. Test: forced hash mismatch → skeleton closed aborted, no orphan.
cmd/ethos/hook.go: the existing audit-log writer call site now wraps its write in session.Store.WithSessionLock so the roster and audit log share one flock per session. Inline comment documents the elimination of the prior two-lock acquisition order.
make check clean — go vet, staticcheck, shellcheck, markdownlint, all tests with -race -count=1, validate-content. No suppression.
Commit incrementally. Each commit passes make check.
Close ceremony FIRST. Write result.yaml and reflect.yaml templates early in the session; submit them BEFORE the 13-minute mark. The previous two missions needed leader intervention or post-hoc ceremony — break that pattern.
